ddls:
CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the customer */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the customer */,
  "married" INTEGER /* Whether the customer is married */,
  "dependents" INTEGER /* Whether the customer has dependents */,
  "annual_income" INTEGER /* Annual income of the customer */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* Education level of the customer */,
  "occupation" TEXT /* Occupation of the customer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the customer */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the customer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_interactions" (
  "interaction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each interaction */,
  "customer_id" TEXT /* ID of the customer interacting */,
  "interaction_date" TEXT /* Date of the interaction */,
  "interaction_type" TEXT /* Type of interaction (e.g., purchase, complaint) */,
  "interaction_detail" TEXT /* Details of the interaction */,
  "interaction_channel" TEXT /* Channel of interaction (e.g., phone, email, in-person) */,
  "interaction_outcome" TEXT /* Outcome of the interaction (e.g., resolved, pending, cancelled) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("interaction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_interactions_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "products" (
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product */,
  "product_name" TEXT /* Name of the product */,
  "product_category" TEXT /* Category of the product */,
  "product_description" TEXT /* Description of the product */,
  "product_price" REAL /* Price of the product */,
  "product_rating" REAL /* Average rating of the product */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sales" (
  "sale_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sale */,
  "customer_id" TEXT /* ID of the customer making the purchase */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product sold */,
  "sale_date" TEXT /* Date of the sale */,
  "sale_amount" REAL /* Amount of the sale */,
  "sale_channel" TEXT /* Channel of sale (e.g., online, in-person) */,
  "sale_discount" REAL /* Discount applied to the sale */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sale_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sales_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sales_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_segments" (
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each segment */,
  "segment_name" TEXT /* Name of the segment */,
  "segment_criteria" TEXT /* Criteria for assigning customers to the segment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("segment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_segment_assignments" (
  "customer_id" TEXT /* ID of the customer */,
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the segment assigned to the customer */,
  "assignment_date" TEXT /* Date of the assignment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id", "segment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "marketing_campaigns" (
  "campaign_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each campaign */,
  "campaign_name" TEXT /* Name of the campaign */,
  "campaign_type" TEXT /* Type of campaign (e.g., email, social media) */,
  "campaign_budget" REAL /* Budget for the campaign */,
  "campaign_start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the campaign */,
  "campaign_end_date" TEXT /* End date of the campaign */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("campaign_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "campaign_targeting" (
  "campaign_id" INTEGER /* ID of the campaign */,
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the segment targeted by the campaign */,
  "targeting_criteria" TEXT /* Additional targeting criteria (e.g., demographics, behavior) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("campaign_id", "segment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sales_forecasts" (
  "forecast_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each forecast */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product being forecasted */,
  "forecast_date" TEXT /* Date of the forecast */,
  "forecast_amount" REAL /* Amount of the forecast */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("forecast_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sales_forecasts_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id")
)
column_names: Channel of interaction (e.g., phone, email, in-person)
Outcome of the interaction (e.g., resolved, pending, cancelled)
ID of the customer making the purchase
Criteria for assigning customers to the segment
ID of the segment assigned to the customer
Type of campaign (e.g., email, social media)
ID of the segment targeted by the campaign
ID of the product being forecasted
